encounter.reason: cycle 4 of 4 induction chemo-immunotherapy for non-small cell lung cancer; final planned induction cycle before maintenance transition
encounter.service_provider: The University of Texas MD Anderson Cancer Center

screening.name: ECOG performance status
screening.interpretation: restricted in physically strenuous activity but ambulatory and able to carry out work of a light or sedentary nature

screening.name: CTCAE fatigue toxicity grade
screening.interpretation: mild fatigue, improved from prior cycle

screening.name: CTCAE peripheral neuropathy toxicity grade
screening.interpretation: improved from grade 2 at cycle 3; numbness limited to fingertip tips bilaterally, not interfering with daily activities

conditions.name: non-small cell lung cancer, stage IIIA (T2b N2 M0), adenocarcinoma of right upper lobe
chemotherapy-induced peripheral neuropathy
conditions.note: improved from grade 2 to grade 1 on gabapentin 300 mg QHS; numbness now limited to fingertip tips and not interfering with daily activities

conditions.name: cancer-related fatigue
conditions.note: improved to grade 1, more manageable; patient has resumed walking one mile daily

medications.name: pembrolizumab 200 mg intravenous infusion
medications.sig: 200 mg IV, infused once, cycle 4 of induction
medications.reason: non-small cell lung cancer, stage IIIA

medications.name: carboplatin 338 mg intravenous infusion (25% dose reduction)
medications.sig: 338 mg IV, infused once, cycle 4 of induction; continued at 25% dose reduction from cycle 3 modification for neuropathy
medications.reason: non-small cell lung cancer, stage IIIA

medications.name: pemetrexed 860 mg intravenous infusion
medications.sig: 860 mg IV, infused once, cycle 4 of induction
medications.reason: non-small cell lung cancer, stage IIIA

medications.name: vitamin B12 (cyanocobalamin) 1000 mcg intramuscular injection
medications.sig: 1000 mcg IM, once, per pemetrexed supplementation protocol (every 9 weeks)

document_references.description: Infusion encounter note documenting cycle 4 (final induction) of pembrolizumab + carboplatin + pemetrexed for stage IIIA NSCLC. Includes pre-treatment assessment with improved neuropathy and fatigue, pre-treatment labs and vitals confirming treatment eligibility, medication administration without infusion reactions, ECOG and CTCAE toxicity assessment, and plan for interim restaging CT on March 24 with transition to maintenance pembrolizumab if adequate response.

Documents administration details for pembrolizumab 200 mg IV, carboplatin 338 mg IV (reduced dose), pemetrexed 860 mg IV, and vitamin B12 1000 mcg IM. Records infusion times, vital sign stability during infusion, and absence of infusion reactions.

diagnostic_reports.description: CMP performed pre-treatment; only creatinine, ALT, AST, and albumin values individually reported. Full panel was reviewed and found acceptable for treatment (renal and hepatic function adequate). Remaining components not individually documented in the narrative.
